summary: Matched the remaining function in ftKb_SpecialNPk by making small, non-semantic C-shaping changes inside ftKb_SsSpecialNHold_Anim. The patch introduced extra Fighter pointer aliases, adjusted PAD_STACK sizing, and used the alias for later charge-state accesses to reach a 100% match for the function while deferring broader linking because the file is split from ftKb_Init.

key_files.change: Only changed file; adjusted locals, stack padding, and Fighter pointer usage in the animation callback.

what_changed: Added extra Fighter pointer locals fp2 and fp3, with fp3 assigned through fp3 = (fp2 = fp).
Changed PAD_STACK from 28 bytes to 0x18 bytes.
Changed the later charge-counter and charge-cap logic to access Fighter state through fp3 instead of fp, including mv.kb.specialhi.x4, fv.kb.xA8, and the ftCo_800BFFD0 call.
Left the earlier command-variable and sound-index logic on fp, preserving the source-level behavior while changing compiler-visible pointer lifetimes.
Did not add broader linking work; the PR body says linking looked hard because this is a split file from ftKb_Init.
smart_moves: Used redundant Fighter pointer aliases as a targeted register/lifetime matching tactic rather than changing the function logic.
Kept the patch scoped to one function and one file, which was enough for the decomp.dev report to show ftKb_SsSpecialNHold_Anim reaching 100.00%.
Separated the source match from the harder file-linking problem instead of blocking the function match on split-file cleanup.
Adjusted PAD_STACK by the exact small amount needed for the match, reducing the prior padding from decimal 28 to 0x18.
decomp_lessons: For stubborn final fighter-function matches, introducing apparently redundant aliases can be the decisive way to reproduce the original compiler's register allocation.
Which local pointer is used for later struct field accesses can matter even when all aliases point to the same Fighter object.
Stack padding should be treated as part of the match surface; small PAD_STACK changes can accompany local-lifetime fixes.
Split files from a larger original unit can make linking harder than matching an individual function, so document the split-origin constraint and land the match separately when appropriate.
Automated before/after decomp reports are useful evidence: this PR's bot report listed one new match and ftKb_SsSpecialNHold_Anim moving from 97.36% to 100.00%.
naming_conventions: Kirby fighter code uses the ftKb_ prefix, as in ftKb_SsSpecialNHold_Anim and ftKb_ChargeShot_inline.
Fighter object locals follow the common fp naming pattern after GET_FIGHTER(gobj).
Kirby data attributes are accessed as ftKb_DatAttrs* da = fp->dat_attrs.
Kirby motion-state constants use the ftKb_MS_ prefix, as in ftKb_MS_SsSpecialNCancel.
Unresolved or external data labels in this area retain address-style names such as ftKb_Init_804D9448 and ftKb_Init_803CB6C0.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Introduce duplicate Fighter* locals, including a second GET_FIGHTER(gobj) local and a chained assignment fp3 = (fp2 = fp), to influence generated code.
Use one alias for the early command/sound logic and another alias for later mv/fv field updates to shape variable lifetimes.
Tune PAD_STACK from 28 to 0x18 to match expected stack layout.
Prefer source-level aliasing and padding adjustments over inline assembly; no asm was introduced in the shown diff.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
The author noted: wanted to also link, but that seemed hard because the file is split from ftKb_Init.
The decomp.dev bot reported GALE01 matched code increased by +0.01% / +456 bytes and listed one new match: main/melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftKb_SpecialNPk ftKb_SsSpecialNHold_Anim from 97.36% to 100.00%.
follow_up_queries: Find other PRs that use redundant Fighter* aliases like fp2 or fp3 to force register allocation.
Find PRs mentioning split files from ftKb_Init or deferred linking work.
Find remaining ftKirby SpecialN or ftKb_SpecialNPk matching/linking tasks.
Compare other PAD_STACK adjustments in fighter animation callbacks.
